subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
sub_strand: ORGANISING, MANAGING AND PRESENTING INFORMATION USING ESSENTIAL PRODUCTIVITY TOOLS
content_standard: 1.1.1.CS.I Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the use of various computing tools to responsibly create multimedia documents containing text, images etc.
indicator_description: Describe software, hardware, and other computing features needed to create a document.
examplars: 1. Brainstorm and discuss: Using mixed-ability/gender-sensitive groups, discuss computer hardware and software components required for creating multimedia documents.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
sub_strand: ORGANISING, MANAGING AND PRESENTING INFORMATION USING ESSENTIAL PRODUCTIVITY TOOLS
content_standard: 1.1.1.CS.I Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the use of various computing tools to responsibly create multimedia documents containing text, images etc.
indicator_description: Create, Edit and Format text and paragraphs (Cut, Copy, Bold, Italic, Tables, Find and Replace, Format Painter, Spelling & Grammar check, Styles) using desktop/online computing tools.
examplars:
1. Discuss the reasons for creating, editing and formatting a document.
2. Project- and Problem-Based Learning and Practical sessions: Engage in project-based learning and practical sessions to create, edit, and format educational artefacts using word processing software.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
sub_strand: ORGANISING, MANAGING AND PRESENTING INFORMATION USING ESSENTIAL PRODUCTIVITY TOOLS
content_standard: 1.1.1.CS.I Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the use of various computing tools to responsibly create multimedia documents containing text, images etc.
indicator_description: Enhance lists by sorting, renumbering, and customising list styles using desktop/online computing tools.
examplars: 1. Participate in discussions and practical sessions to explore and apply formatting tools (e.g., bullets, numbering, sorting, alignment) in word processing to enhance lists.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
sub_strand: ORGANISING, MANAGING AND PRESENTING INFORMATION USING ESSENTIAL PRODUCTIVITY TOOLS
content_standard: 1.1.1.CS.I Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the use of various computing tools to responsibly create multimedia documents containing text, images etc.
indicator_description: Format the overall appearance of a page through colours, watermarks, headers and footers, page layout, paragraph and line, change capitalisation, bordering and shading, perform bullets and numbering, do page column, tables, graphics).
examplars: 1. Discuss, present, and demonstrate the use of various formatting features in word processing software to format the overall appearance of a page.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
sub_strand: ORGANISING, MANAGING AND PRESENTING INFORMATION USING ESSENTIAL PRODUCTIVITY TOOLS
content_standard: 1.1.1.CS.I Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the use of various computing tools to responsibly create multimedia documents containing text, images etc.
indicator_description: Save and print documents (Save, Save As, Print).
examplars: 1. Use laptops or PCs to demonstrate and practice saving and printing documents, showcasing the steps involved.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 1.1.2.CSI Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Emerging Technologies.
indicator_description: Explain digital devices and their importance.
examplars: 1. Project-Based Learning: Engage in individual and mixed-ability/gender-sensitive group work to brainstorm and explain the importance of digital devices.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 1.1.2.CSI Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Emerging Technologies.
indicator_description: Differentiate between desktops, laptops, smart gadgets (tablets), and servers.
examplars: 1. Use videos or pictures to differentiate between desktops, laptops, smart gadgets (tablets), and servers. Encourage learners to organise their thoughts by creating a table or comparison chart.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 1.1.2.CSI Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Emerging Technologies.
indicator_description: Explore the purpose and uses of smartphones, digital cameras, wearable devices, game devices, e-book readers, portable and digital media players.
examplars: 1. Project- and Problem-Based Learning and Practical Sessions: Engage in project-based and problem-based learning sessions to explore the purpose and uses of smartphones, digital cameras, wearable devices, game devices, e-book readers, and portable digital media players.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 1.1.3.CS.I Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the use of the Internet.
indicator_description: Describe the evolution of the Internet and identify the Internet service providers and their functions.
examplars:
1. Engage in interactive multimedia presentations to describe the evolution of the internet.
2. Analyse and discuss the presentations in mixed-ability groups.
3. In group discussions, identify and explore the functions of different types of internet service providers (ISPs).

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 1.1.3.CS.I Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the use of the Internet.
indicator_description: Explore how data travels on the Internet, how the IP addressing system functions and discuss surfing the Web, creating and use of e-mail and mailing lists.
examplars:
1. Present and demonstrate how data travels on the internet, allowing learners to explore and practice in groups.
2. Provide a multimedia presentation and demonstration on IP addressing, explaining its types and usage.
3. Engage in discussions on Web surfing (internet browsing) and conduct demonstrations and practice sessions for creating email accounts and using mailing lists.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 1.2.1.CSI Demonstrate basic knowledge and understanding of guided and unguided network systems.
indicator_description: Classify network types and topologies.
examplars:
1. Engage in interactive learner-centred teaching and learning to explain different network topologies, such as star, ring, bus, and mesh.
2. Utilise multimedia presentations and video analysis to discuss the concepts and techniques of both wired and wireless networks.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 1.2.1.CSI Demonstrate basic knowledge and understanding of guided and unguided network systems.
indicator_description: Classify transmission media used to send and receive data in a network environment.
examplars:
1. Engage in group work to discuss and differentiate network types.
2. Implement project-based learning and practical sessions to explore and connect transmission media.
3. Utilise interactive multimedia presentations and video analysis to discuss the concepts and techniques of both wired and wireless networks.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 1.2.2.CS.I Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Safety and Security Issues in the use of computers.
indicator_description: Discuss Physical Safety Risks (Electrocution, Fire hazard, Tripping hazard, Personal injury) in the use of computers.
examplars:
1. Conduct presentations and engage in mixed-ability group discussions to explore and discuss physical safety risks associated with computer use.
2. Enhance understanding through multimedia presentations that showcase real-life occurrences of these risks.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 1.2.2.CS.I Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Safety and Security Issues in the use of computers.
indicator_description: Discuss Causes of Physical Safety Risks of computer equipment.
examplars:
1. Facilitate discussions and presentations among learners, to examine the causes of physical safety risks associated with computer equipment, with a specific focus on the issue of overheating due to poor heat dissipation.
2. Encourage learners to analyse the factors contributing to this risk and discuss preventive measures.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 1.2.2.CS.I Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Safety and Security Issues in the use of computers.
indicator_description: Apply preventive measures on physical safety risks (Increase the number of wall sockets and do not use too many extension blocks).
examplars:
1. Engage in Think-Pair-Share activities to brainstorm and discuss various preventive measures for physical safety risks in the use and handling of computers.
2. Encourage discussions on preventive measures applicable in different contexts, such as our society, schools, and homes.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
sub_strand: ORGANISING, MANAGING AND PRESENTING INFORMATION USING ESSENTIAL PRODUCTIVITY TOOLS
content_standard: 2.1.1.CSI Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Analysing numerical data using Spreadsheet software.
indicator_description: Describe Spreadsheet software workbooks, worksheets, cell referencing.
examplars:
1. Whole class discussion: Participate in a class discussion on spreadsheet software, considering its key features and components.
2. Engage in project-based learning activities to explore and utilise the features of spreadsheet software.
3. Interactive multimedia presentations, and video analysis to discuss the concepts and explore techniques for creating electronic spreadsheets.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
sub_strand: ORGANISING, MANAGING AND PRESENTING INFORMATION USING ESSENTIAL PRODUCTIVITY TOOLS
content_standard: 2.1.1.CSI Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Analysing numerical data using Spreadsheet software.
indicator_description: Create and utilise formulae and functions for data analysis.
examplars:
1. Work on practical assignments, individually or in groups, to create and use formulae and functions in spreadsheet software.
2. Utilise various built-in functions, such as SUM, AVERAGE, MIN, MAX, IF, DATE, and TIME, to analyse numerical data effectively.
3. Participate in interactive multimedia presentations and video analysis to deepen understanding of creating and using formulae and functions.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
sub_strand: ORGANISING, MANAGING AND PRESENTING INFORMATION USING ESSENTIAL PRODUCTIVITY TOOLS
content_standard: 2.1.1.CSI Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Analysing numerical data using Spreadsheet software.
indicator_description: Generate and interpret graphs and charts to visualise data.
examplars:
1. Engage in project-based learning activities to generate different types of graphs and charts, such as bar charts/graphs, pie charts, and line graphs, using spreadsheet software.
2. Demonstrate the ability to interpret and analyse data presented in graphical formats.
3. Utilise interactive multimedia presentations and video analysis to explore concepts and techniques related to creating graphs and charts.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
sub_strand: ORGANISING, MANAGING AND PRESENTING INFORMATION USING ESSENTIAL PRODUCTIVITY TOOLS
content_standard: 2.1.1.CSI Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Analysing numerical data using Spreadsheet software.
indicator_description: Apply sorting and filtering operations to manipulate tables.
examplars:
1. Apply sorting operations (ascending, descending) to arrange data in tables effectively.
2. Utilise filtering operations to display specific data subsets based on given criteria.
3. Engage in project-based learning activities to practise and demonstrate the application of sorting and filtering operations in spreadsheet software.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
sub_strand: ORGANISING, MANAGING AND PRESENTING INFORMATION USING ESSENTIAL PRODUCTIVITY TOOLS
content_standard: 2.1.1.CSI Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Analysing numerical data using Spreadsheet software.
indicator_description: Save and print workbooks and worksheets (Save, Save As, Print).
examplars:
1. Expose learners to how to save workbooks and worksheets using appropriate file formats.
2. Help them to explore how to organise and manage spreadsheet files and folders effectively.
3. Practice printing workbooks and worksheets, utilising features such as print-preview and page-setup options.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 2.1.2.CSI Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Emerging Technologies.
indicator_description: Discuss the use of technology in education, healthcare, manufacturing, government, finance, entertainment, transport, and business.
examplars:
1. Co-operative Learning: Using mixed-ability groups, discuss the applications of technology in education, healthcare, manufacturing, government, finance, entertainment, transport, and business.
2. Utilise interactive multimedia presentations and video analysis to explore and assess the merits and challenges of technology in different sectors.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 2.1.2.CSI Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Emerging Technologies.
indicator_description: Identify technologies used by home users, small/home office users, mobile users, power users and enterprise users.
examplars:
1. Participate in project-based and problem-based learning activities to identify technologies commonly used by home users, small/home office users, mobile users, power users, and enterprise users.
2. Utilise inclusive teaching strategies, such as multimedia presentations, to ensure the understanding and engagement of diverse learners.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 2.1.3.CS.I Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the World Wide Web.
indicator_description: Navigate the Web effectively using Web addresses, browsers, Web apps, and mobile platforms.
examplars:
1. Think-Pair-Share: Engage in a Think-Pair-Share activity to discuss the purpose and components of Web addresses (Uniform Resource Locators), Web browsers, Web apps, and mobile platforms.
2. Problem-Based Learning and Practical Session: Participate in problem-based learning and practical sessions to practice using Web addresses, different Web browsers, Web apps, and mobile platforms to search for and communicate information.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 2.1.3.CS.I Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the World Wide Web.
indicator_description: Analyse and discuss the features and multimedia content found on Websites (Graphics, Audio, Videos, Plug-ins).
examplars:
1. Collaborate with a partner to discuss different websites, their examples, and their relevance in various contexts.
2. Brainstorm and explore the different types of digital media found on websites, such as graphics, audio, videos, and plug-ins.
3. Demonstrate for learners the use of different digital media for communication purposes, including text, voice, and video communication, virtual meetings, email, instant messaging, and screen sharing.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 2.2.1.CS.I Demonstrate basic knowledge and understanding of guided and unguided network systems.
indicator_description: Understand the Concepts and Differences Between Client/Server and Peer-to-Peer Networks.
examplars:
1. Engage in interactive learner-centred teaching and learning activities to discuss and analyse the characteristics, advantages and limitations of Client/Server and Peer-to-Peer Networks.
2. Participate in group discussions and case studies to explore real-world examples of Client/Server and Peer-to-Peer Networks and their applications.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 2.2.1.CS.I Demonstrate basic knowledge and understanding of guided and unguided network systems.
indicator_description: Describe and Explain Common Network Communication Protocols and Standards, such as Ethernet, TCP/IP, HTTP(s), SMTP, FTP, Wi-Fi, LTE, Bluetooth, IrDA, RFID, and NFC.
examplars:
1. Engage in interactive learner-centred teaching and learning activities to describe and explain network communication protocols and standards, including their purposes, features, and functionalities.
2. Conduct an inquiry and research project to investigate specific examples of network communication protocols and standards and their applications in various contexts.
3. Participate in group discussions with the ability to understand and evaluate multiple features, discussing the importance and reasons for using communication protocols and standards.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 2.2.2.CS.I Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of safety and security issues in ICT.
indicator_description: Understand and evaluate the risks associated with the use of ICT, including Internet and network attacks (e.g., cyberbullying, malware, botnets, denial-of-service attacks, spoofing, hardware theft, and firewalls).
examplars:
1. Engage in group discussions and presentations to examine real-life examples of internet and network attacks, their impact, and the ethical implications associated with them.
2. Conduct research and analysis on specific types of attacks, their methods, and potential consequences.
3. Participate in radio presentations or other forms of communication to raise awareness about internet and network attacks and their prevention.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 2.2.2.CS.I Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of safety and security issues in ICT.
indicator_description: Discuss and implement preventive mechanisms to mitigate computer network attacks (e.g., antivirus software, hardware and software, and firewalls).
examplars:
1. Engage in brainstorming sessions to explore various preventive mechanisms and strategies for countering network attacks.
2. Participate in group discussions and presentations to analyse the effectiveness of preventive mechanisms, such as antivirus software and firewalls.
3. Discuss and share best practices for securing computer networks, including the responsible use of ICT and adherence to ethical guidelines.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
sub_strand: ORGANISING, MANAGING AND PRESENTING INFORMATION USING ESSENTIAL PRODUCTIVITY TOOLS
content_standard: 3.1.1.CSI Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Presentation software and Desktop Publishing Software.
indicator_description: Discuss Presentation Software and its Features (Windows, Animation, Transition, Presenter view slides, Master slides).
examplars:
1. Engage in brainstorming sessions to explore different presentation software, examples, such as MS PowerPoint, Google Slides, Prezi, Lotus Freelance, Ludus, Open Office Impress, Slide Bean, etc.
2. Participate in group discussions to evaluate the merits and demerits of using presentation software.
3. Analyse the features of presentation software, including Windows, Animation, Transitions, Presenter view slides, and Master slides.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
sub_strand: ORGANISING, MANAGING AND PRESENTING INFORMATION USING ESSENTIAL PRODUCTIVITY TOOLS
content_standard: 3.1.1.CSI Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Presentation software and Desktop Publishing Software.
indicator_description: Develop slides, format tables, and incorporate charts, graphs, animation, and transitions in a slide presentation.
examplars:
1. Work individually or in mixed-ability groups to create slide presentations on a topic of learners' choice, applying formatting techniques, and using tables, charts, graphs, animation, and transitions.
2. Engage in interactive multimedia presentations and video analysis to learn and explore techniques for formatting tables, creating charts and graphs, and incorporating animation and transitions.
3. Use project-based learning to apply animation, transitions, and other features to enhance the visual appeal and effectiveness of slide presentations.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
sub_strand: ORGANISING, MANAGING AND PRESENTING INFORMATION USING ESSENTIAL PRODUCTIVITY TOOLS
content_standard: 3.1.1.CSI Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Presentation software and Desktop Publishing Software.
indicator_description: Create and save a publication using desktop publishing software, such as MS Publisher or Adobe.
examplars:
1. Engage in group discussions to understand the features and uses of desktop publishing software, such as MS Publisher and Adobe.
2. Discuss the steps involved in creating and saving a publication, including working with text, adding building blocks, and utilising various tools.
3. Apply project-based learning within practical sessions to create and save text, add building blocks, and utilise the tools in desktop publishing software.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
sub_strand: ORGANISING, MANAGING AND PRESENTING INFORMATION USING ESSENTIAL PRODUCTIVITY TOOLS
content_standard: 3.1.1.CSI Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Presentation software and Desktop Publishing Software.
indicator_description: Utilise basic tools in desktop publishing to add pictures, text boxes, tables, word art, and picture frames to a publication.
examplars:
1. Engage in discussions to understand and explore the basic tools used in desktop publishing, such as text boxes, tables, word art, and picture frames.
2. Participate in project-based learning activities to practice using these tools and enhance publications with visual elements.
3. Embrace diversity and practice inclusion by creating publications that accommodate different abilities and promote accessibility.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 3.1.2.CS.I Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of emerging technologies.
indicator_description: Analyse and Discuss the Affordances and Impact of Digital Tools in Various Domains.
examplars:
1. Engage in interactive multimedia presentations and video analysis to explore and discuss the use of emerging technologies in education, healthcare, manufacturing, government, finance, entertainment, transport, and business.
2. Investigate and analyse the benefits and limitations of digital tools in different contexts, such as enhancing productivity, enabling remote collaboration, facilitating data analysis, etc.
3. Reflect on the ethical considerations and potential societal impacts of emerging technologies.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 3.1.2.CS.I Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of emerging technologies.
indicator_description: Evaluate the Role of Social Media as Communication and Collaboration Tools in Society. (Example Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube, etc.)
examplars:
1. Participate in Think-Pair-Share activities and discussions to examine the role of social media platforms like Facebook, Twitter / X, Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube, and other relevant platforms as tools for communication and collaboration.
2. Research to understand the purposes, features, and current trends of different social media platforms.
3. Share personal experiences and insights regarding specific social media platforms they have used, discussing the advantages, disadvantages, and potential impact on individuals and society.
4. Engage in discussions that explore the effects of social media on privacy, mental health, relationships, information sharing, and societal dynamics.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 3.1.3.CSI Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Collaboration tools for Communication and synchronisation.
indicator_description: Create and Set up Communication Tools (Synchronous (Real-time) and/or Asynchronous Communication) (Zoom, Google Meet, Facebook, other video conferencing platforms, instant messaging, telephone conversation / Email, messengers, Intranet, etc.)
examplars:
1. Identify and select appropriate communication tools for different scenarios, considering factors such as real-time or asynchronous communication, the nature of the information being shared, and the target audience.
2. Set up and configure communication tools such as Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, other video conferencing platforms, instant messaging applications, email systems, and intranets.
3. Participate in problem-based learning and practical sessions, both individually and in group work, to engage in effective communication using collaboration tools.
4. Demonstrate proficiency in using Web browsers, Web Apps, and mobile platforms to facilitate communication and collaboration.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 3.1.3.CSI Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Collaboration tools for Communication and synchronisation.
indicator_description: Discuss the Types of Websites and Digital Media on the Web (Graphics, Audio, Videos, Plug-ins)
examplars:
1. Discuss and analyse the different types of Websites, including informational, e-commerce, social networking, and multimedia-based Websites.
2. Identify and evaluate various forms of digital media, such as graphics, audio files, videos, and plug-ins, and their roles in enhancing communication and user engagement.
3. Practice utilising text, voice, and video communication in virtual meetings, email exchanges, instant messaging conversations, and screen-sharing activities to effectively communicate information and ideas.
4. Apply appropriate digital media elements to enhance the clarity and impact of communication materials.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 3.2.1.CSI Demonstrate basic knowledge and understanding of guided and unguided network systems.
indicator_description: Describe Commonly used Devices (e.g., broadband, modems, wireless access points, routers, network cards, hubs and switches).
examplars:
1. Engage in interactive and learner-centred teaching and learning activities to explore and understand the purpose and function of network devices, such as broadband, modems, wireless access points, routers, network cards, hubs, and switches.
2. Conduct brainstorming sessions to discuss the significance of these devices in our society and daily lives, highlighting their roles in enabling network communication and connectivity.
3. Analyse and compare the characteristics, advantages, and limitations of different network devices to develop a comprehensive understanding of their applications and usage scenarios.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 3.2.2.CS.I Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Safety and security Issues in ICT.
indicator_description: Discuss the Fundamental Objectives of Computer Security.
examplars:
1. Engage in interactive and learner-centered teaching and learning activities to explore the fundamental objectives of computer security.
2. Conduct brainstorming sessions to explore the fundamental objectives of computer security.
3. Analyse the fundamental objectives of computer security.

subject: INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATIONS TECHNOLOGY
content_standard: 3.2.2.CS.I Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of Safety and security Issues in ICT.
indicator_description: Discuss the Threats to Computer Security and how to Prevent such Threats.
examplars:
1. In different mixed-ability groups, task learners to surf the net and discuss the threats to computer security and how to prevent such threats.
2. Encourage respect and tolerance of all opinions and encourage all learners to share ideas.
